other_observers: Nick Komar, Eric Defonso, Sue Riffe, Dan Lazlo, and 4 other people which I never got their names.; Nick Komar found the first bird that we saw, but we had heard from Connie Kogler that she had seen two of them earilier in the day before we got there.
duration: We watched the bird for about 15 minutes and watched the other birds for about 3 minutes each
specific_location: Crow Valley Campground, a few hundred yards north of the picnic area was where the first one was. Then all the others were spread out to the west of the picnic area.
description: The bird had an overall gray color, including the tail. The head was even a little darker gray than the body. It had gray on the cheeks. It had extensive spotting on the breast. It had dark gray flanks. It had a small eye ring. The front of the lower mandible was black, while the rest of the lower mandible was yellow. All the other birds looked the same.Behaviors: The first bird wsa on the ground most of the time by a dripping water fountain. It would occasionally fly up to the trees in the area and stay there for a few minutes, then it would drop back down to the ground.Call: Didn't hear it.Plumage: Adult
